summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures an acoustic‑set masterpiece with standout versions of “I’ve Been All Around This World” and a fiery third‑set jam.
review: The Wise/Miller audience tape is praised for its remarkable clarity, especially on the transcendent acoustic set, while still preserving the live ambience. The performance shines with several peak moments – the all‑around version of “I’ve Been All Around This World,” a scorching “Greatest Story Ever Told,” a complete “Friend of the Devil,” and a high‑energy third set featuring “Bertha” and “Johnny B. Goode.” Minor slips (tempo loss on “Monkey & the Engineer,” missed verses on “Rosa Lee McFall”) keep it from being flawless, but overall the show is celebrated as one of the best 1980 recordings.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight both superb sound quality and many standout performances; only a few minor criticisms lower the average slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear imaging with spectacular acoustic set detail; occasional audience chatter, brief tape‑flip noise and minor tempo/verse glitches on a handful of songs.

standout_songs: I’ve Been All Around This World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set show: an acoustic first set that peaks early, a solid second set, and a third set that builds to high‑energy jams with strong finishes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s high‑lonesome solos are praised on “Fire” and “Johnny B. Goode,” though he missed verses in “Rosa Lee McFall.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is highlighted for his solo on “Johnny B. Goode” and noted as trimmed on the official release of “Friend of the Devil.”
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers receive strong praise for fire, tight 16th‑note fills, and overall energy throughout especially in the third set.
